regulatory_events.title: Pentagon labels Anthropic as supply-chain risk
regulatory_events.summary: Department of Defense officially designated Anthropic as a supply-chain risk after failed contract negotiations over military AI usage restrictions.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with native computer control
top_stories.why_it_matters: This represents the first major AI model capable of autonomously operating desktop applications, marking the transition from conversational AI to truly autonomous agents that can perform complex workflows.

top_stories.headline: Pentagon designates Anthropic as supply-chain risk
top_stories.why_it_matters: The official DoD designation creates a precedent for how AI companies that refuse military contracts may be treated, potentially impacting the broader AI industry's relationship with government.

top_stories.headline: Block fires 4,000 employees after AI productivity surge
top_stories.why_it_matters: This represents one of the first major corporate layoffs directly attributed to AI productivity gains, with the company's stock price surging in response, signaling how markets may reward AI-driven workforce reductions.

one_to_watch.headline: Legal AI adoption doubles while firms lag behind individual practitioners
one_to_watch.why_it_matters: The 70% individual adoption rate versus slower firm-wide implementation suggests a grassroots legal tech revolution that could force institutional changes in how legal services are delivered.
